mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation expectations, which forces equity valuations to contract as discount rates rise, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly UNRATE increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply-side bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive or flat during late-cycle INDPRO declines, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher GDPC1 growth rates reflect robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue projections and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, suppressing investment and consumption, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS crosses 4.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST during a post-shock recovery phase signals collapsing housing demand, which triggers financial sector stress and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive following large HOUST drops in recovery regimes, or the signal shows no regime-dependent predictive power.
